repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md ExactID catalogue has no completed DML/HTE entry, and eid_dml_orth_uniform_v2 targets DML/LRO/ADML studentization-report equivalence rather than this HTE remainder expansion

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML and SemenovaChernozhukov2021DMLCATE: AIPW/orthogonal-score exact-ID identities and sufficient DML expansion conditions

repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest eid_dml_orth_uniform_v2 is a rejected report-studentization frontier, not a Holder/entropy HTE first-order orthogonality breakdown frontier

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML gives sufficient orthogonal-score product-rate conditions; FosterSyrgkanis2023OSL gives excess-risk bounds; SemenovaChernozhukov2021DMLCATE gives simultaneous CATE-summary inference, but none states the iff B_n frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue Holder exponent DML/HTE frontier

published_prior_art.cite: KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE and ArmstrongKolesar2021FiniteSampleATE establish smoothness elbows/limits; the displayed exponent is mainly the Holder-rate specialization of Conjecture 1

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The load-bearing Assumption curvature lower bound scales the deterministic bilinear drift sup by E_n, but with uniformly bounded report weights |R_P(ell;u,v)| is bounded at order delta_g delta_p, so growing entropy cannot multiply the population drift without a different normalization or moving that factor to an empirical-process term.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The Holder exponent boundary r_g+r_p>1/2+zeta inherits the same entropy-multiplied deterministic-remainder issue from Conjecture 1; as written, zeta enters the frontier through an assumption that appears incompatible with bounded report weights.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the curvature condition with a norm/normalization under which the report-class supremum can genuinely scale with E_n, or remove E_n from the population bilinear drift and put entropy only in the empirical-process remainder.
Add a one-line inequality in Section 9 for the Haar/wavelet witness showing exactly how the proposed L_n normalization evades the bounded-envelope |R_P| <= C delta_g delta_p obstruction.

Name the specific Gaussian-approximation theorem planned for the oracle process, such as a high-dimensional Gaussian comparison or empirical-process maximal inequality, and state which entropy growth regime it permits.

recommended_next_step: Patch the entropy/remainder normalization before Stage 0; the novelty kernel is plausible, but the current B_n frontier is not mathematically well-posed.
